fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Delaware Occupational Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as an occupational therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Delaware - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: occupational therapist license reciprocity delaware, how to transfer occupational therapist license to delaware, occupational therapist compact delaware, delaware occupational therapist endorsement application

board.name: Delaware Board of Occupational Therapy Practice
board.mailing_address:
Delaware Division of Professional Regulation
Cannon Building
861 Silver Lake Blvd., Suite 203
Dover, DE 19904

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Certification in Occupational Therapy (NBCOT) examination
quick_facts.ce_requirement: 24 contact hours of continuing education per renewal cycle

license_types.title: Occupational Therapist Registered (OTR)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice occupational therapy independently in Delaware.

license_types.title: Certified Occupational Therapy Assistant (COTA)
license_types.description: Requires an associate degree and practicing under the supervision of a licensed OTR.

steps.title: Create a DELPROS Account
steps.description: Access the DELPROS (Delaware Professional Regulation Online Services) portal at https://delpros.delaware.gov/ and register for a new user account if you do not already have one.

steps.title: Submit Online Application
steps.description: Log in to DELPROS, select "New Application" for Occupational Therapist licensure, and complete all required sections of the application, including uploading any necessary documents.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the non-refundable $160 application fee directly through the DELPROS system.

steps.title: Complete Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow instructions within DELPROS to schedule and complete a state and federal criminal background check. You will pay a separate fee to the state police for this service.

steps.title: Arrange for Third-Party Verifications
steps.description: Request official transcripts from your OT program, official NBCOT score verification, and license verifications from all states where you hold or have held an OT license. These must be sent directly to the Delaware Board of Occupational Therapy Practice from the issuing institutions/boards.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly log in to your DELPROS account to monitor the status of your application and respond promptly to any requests for additional information from the Board.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBCOT exam.

provisional_text: Delaware offers a temporary occupational therapy permit for new graduates who have completed an ACOTE-accredited program and are awaiting their NBCOT exam results. The temporary permit allows practice under the direct supervision of a licensed OTR. Contact the Delaware Board of Occupational Therapy Practice for current temporary permit requirements and duration limits.
reciprocity_text: Delaware is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Delaware without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Delaware also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

faqs.question: How much does a Delaware OT license cost?
faqs.answer: The total initial application and licensing fee is approximately $236. Check the board website for the latest fee schedule.

faqs.question: Is NBCOT certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBCOT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across all states.

faqs.question: Does Delaware have reciprocity for occupational therapists?
faqs.answer: Delaware has enacted the OT Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, occupational therapists still apply by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my occupational therapist license to Delaware?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Delaware while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Delaware is a member of the OT Licensure Compact (OTC). Occupational therapists holding an active compact privilege may practice in Delaware without a separate state license. For those not eligible for compact privileges, Delaware also accepts standard endorsement applications with proof of current NBCOT certification and license verification from your home state.

hero.title: Become an Occupational Therapist in Delaware
hero.dek: To apply for an Occupational Therapist license in Delaware, navigate to the DELPROS (Delaware Professional Regulation Online Services) portal and begin your online application.

official_links.registration_steps:
1. Visit the DELPROS portal at https://delpros.delaware.gov/.
2. Click the "Register a New User" button.
3. Complete the registration form, verify your email address, and create your login credentials.
4. Log in to your newly created DELPROS account.
